title: Package RBF
description: Current Bitcoin Core package RBF supports limited 1P1C-style replacement of conflicting in-mempool transaction packages when the replacement satisfies aggregate-fee, incremental-fee, cluster-limit, and feerate-improvement rules, allowing child fees to replace a package rather than only a single transaction.

resources.title: Bitcoin Core Package Mempool Acceptance Policy
resources.notes: Canonical package topology, package-fee, and package-replacement policy context.

resources.title: Bitcoin Core Mempool Replacements
resources.notes: Current replacement conditions for absolute fees, incremental relay fees, conflicting clusters, and feerate-diagram improvement.

resources.title: Bitcoin Core 28 Wallet Integration Guide
resources.notes: Practical 1P1C package-RBF examples showing how a child can carry fees for a replacement package.

resources.title: Mastering Bitcoin
resources.notes: Background for RBF, mempool policy, transaction packages, and fee-bumping tradeoffs.
